message:
fix: extract syscall.SIGUSR2 into platform files for Windows cross-compilation (gt-94i9)

daemon.go:379 used syscall.SIGUSR2 directly, which is undefined on Windows.
Move the signal-sending logic into daemon_reload_unix.go and
daemon_reload_windows.go with appropriate build tags, following the
existing process_unix.go/process_windows.go pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+//go:build !windows
+
+package cmd
+
+import (
+	"os"
+	"syscall"
+)
+
+// signalDaemonReload sends SIGUSR2 to the daemon process to trigger a reload.
+func signalDaemonReload(process *os.Process) error {
+	return process.Signal(syscall.SIGUSR2)
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+//go:build windows
+
+package cmd
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+)
+
+// signalDaemonReload is a no-op on Windows since SIGUSR2 is not available.
+func signalDaemonReload(process *os.Process) error {
+	return fmt.Errorf("daemon reload signal not supported on Windows")
+}
